primary_diagnosis.name: Alopecia areata (rapidly progressive) with trichodynia and acute anxiety/panic

pathophysiology_summary: Alopecia areata is a T-cell mediated autoimmune attack on anagen hair follicles causing well-demarcated non-scarring patches, exclamation-mark hairs and a positive hair-pull test at active margins; it associates with other autoimmunity (here autoimmune thyroid disease) and nail pitting. Follicles are preserved (non-scarring), so regrowth is possible. Trichodynia (scalp burning/dysesthesia) commonly accompanies active shedding and, with health-catastrophising, has precipitated a panic attack. Discriminators from mimics: normal smooth scalp with no scale/pustules/crusts or lymphadenopathy (against tinea capitis/kerion), focal well-demarcated patches with exclamation-mark hairs (against diffuse telogen effluvium), and preserved follicular ostia with no erythema/plugging (against scarring alopecia such as discoid lupus). The dual priorities are correct dermatological diagnosis/reassurance and acute psychological safety.

differential.evidence_basis: Sudden well-demarcated patches, smooth scalp, exclamation-mark hairs, autoimmune thyroid background.
Acute painful scalp with hair loss could be inflammatory tinea; screen for scale/pustules/crusts/nodes.
Must exclude irreversible scarring — look for erythema, follicular plugging, loss of ostia.
Causes shedding but diffuse, without focal patches or exclamation-mark hairs.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Screen for infection/scarring and self-harm; do not anchor on the panic and skip the structured scalp assessment.

differential.evidence_basis: Smooth normal scalp + exclamation-mark hairs confirm non-scarring AA.
No scale, pustules, crusting or lymphadenopathy.
No erythema, follicular plugging or loss of ostia.
Working diagnosis: reassurance about non-scarring nature, acute psychological safety-net, GP follow-up for corticosteroid therapy.

red_flags.description: Scalp erythema, pustules, crusting, boggy swelling or lymphadenopathy (infection/kerion or scarring)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Would change management to antifungal/anti-inflammatory and imply possible irreversible loss; absent here.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missed kerion/scarring alopecia could lead to permanent hair loss.

red_flags.description: Acute severe panic with thoughts of self-harm or inability to breathe/settle
red_flags.clinical_significance: Acute psychiatric crisis in a person living alone requires screening and an emergency safety-net.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Unrecognised self-harm risk or uncontrolled panic could lead to harm or an ED crisis.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Managing only the panic and failing to diagnose or to exclude infection/scarring alopecia.
Newly prescribing a benzodiazepine over telehealth for panic instead of non-pharmacological measures and appropriate escalation/support.
Not asking about safety in an acutely distressed person living alone.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Reassure and screen; provide non-pharmacological panic management and urgent psychological support; escalate to ED/000 if self-harm risk or uncontrolled panic. GP within 3–5 days for AA treatment.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Remote benzodiazepine prescribing, or missing infection/scarring or a self-harm risk, are the main remote-assessment hazards.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Alopecia areata is non-scarring; options include topical/intralesional corticosteroids; address psychological impact and screen associated autoimmune disease.
